purpose: Small human-reviewed seed corpus for beta validation precision checks.

entries.title: Healthy 3.3V I2C sensor with explicit 4.7k pull-ups

expectedNonIssues.notes: SDA and SCL both have visible 4.7k pull-ups to 3.3V.
Power and ground rails are not shorted.

entries.title: Official I2C IC without visible pull-ups

expectedIssues.notes: Official-complete I2C part exposes SDA/SCL but no pull-up path exists.

expectedNonIssues.notes: Missing I2C pull-ups must not be confused with a rail short.

entries.title: Direct 5V to GND short through a synthetic link

expectedIssues.notes: The same component explicitly ties a 5V rail pin to GND.

expectedNonIssues.notes: This sample has no I2C bus.

entries.title: Generic I2C module where onboard pull-ups are unknown

expectedIssues.notes: Generic module status should keep this as a conservative review warning, not a hard error.

expectedNonIssues.notes: Unknown module pull-ups are not a power short.

entries.title: 5V op-amp gain stage driving a 3.3V ESP32 ADC pin

expectedIssues.notes: The gain stage can drive the ADC above its reference range.

expectedNonIssues.notes: The op-amp sample is an analog range issue, not a rail short.

entries.title: LED tied directly between 5V and GND

expectedIssues.notes: A bare LED needs a visible series resistor.

expectedNonIssues.notes: A current-limited LED issue is not a direct rail short.

entries.title: LED with a 220 ohm series resistor

expectedNonIssues.notes: The 220 ohm resistor is in series with the LED.
The series resistor should still allow a normal indicator current.

entries.title: HC-06 RX driven directly from a 5V Arduino GPIO

expectedIssues.notes: HC-06 RX should not be treated as a confirmed-safe 5V input path.

expectedNonIssues.notes: A signal-level mismatch is not a rail short.

entries.title: HC-06 RX protected by a matched level shifter channel

expectedNonIssues.notes: The board GPIO reaches HC-06 RX through a matched level-shifter path.
The HV and LV channel sides are both connected.

entries.title: General op-amp stage without a feedback path

expectedIssues.notes: The op-amp has input and output use but no visible feedback network.

expectedNonIssues.notes: The missing feedback path should not look like a power short.

entries.title: 1M/1M divider feeding an Arduino ADC input directly

expectedIssues.notes: A megaohm divider is too high impedance for a direct ADC source.

expectedNonIssues.notes: The divider is high impedance, not a short.

entries.title: VIN 9V source with 5V rail load near regulator headroom

expectedIssues.notes: The 5V rail is close enough to the budget to warn before hard overload.

expectedNonIssues.notes: This sample should be low headroom, not a full budget failure.

entries.title: Custom fallback-style I2C module with unknown onboard pull-ups

expectedIssues.notes: Generic/custom module status should remain conservative instead of becoming a hard error.

expectedNonIssues.notes: Unknown pull-ups are not a direct short.

entries.title: Unsupported zip import gets a safe failure category
10k/10k divider feeding an Arduino ADC input

expectedNonIssues.notes: The divider source impedance is low enough for this seed sample.
The divider path is resistive, not a rail short.
